'''Tallaso''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="HR" /> situated in the [[Tallaso system/Legends|Tallaso system]], a part of the [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="WesternReaches" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Riggins Delahrg]] served as the Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of Tallaso. He extorted [[Currency/Legends|money]] from Tallaso's native [[Tallaso planetary government|planetary government]] and took bribes from [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] business consortiums and crime syndicates that wished to exploit the planet's resources. Delahrg's position ensured a comfortable life for him and was powerful on a local scale—although it was relatively unimportant in the wider context of the Empire.<ref name="HR" />

After the Empire began to fragment following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="HR" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Imperial support was withdrawn from Tallaso. Subsequently, the planet's populace rose up and ousted Delahrg, leading him to steal anything he could and then flee, with the former governor eventually traveling to the [[Vandron system/Legends|Vandron system]], which had the closest functioning Imperial [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]].<ref name="HR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tallaso was mentioned in the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', authored by [[Rick D. Stuart|Rick Stuart]] and [[Paul Sudlow]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="HR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tallaso [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet, in grid square K-17.<ref name="TEA" />
